### Post by Inxsible on 2007-06-15
This is my fstab
```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
/dev/sda5 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
/dev/sda6 /home ext3 defaults 0 2
/dev/sda3 /shared ext3 defaults 0 2
/dev/sda1 /windows/sda1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 1
/dev/sda2 /media/Windows ntfs-3g rw,auto,exec,user,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 1
/dev/sda7 none swap sw 0 0
#/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
```I want to mount my sda2 and sda3 so that I get drive icons on the desktop. sda2 mounts under /media/Windows but the desktop icon shows "Windows Data" which was the volume label i gave it under Windows.

When I go to /media/Windows ...i cannot see the contents of the drive -probably permissions. But when I click the icon Windows Data, i have full permissions. That works for me, i guess.

sda3 mounts as well under /shared, however I do not get an icon for it on the desktop. I have to go to Computer --> FileSystem --> shared   to access it

How can I change the mount options so that I will get an icon on the desktop just like i get one for sda2 even if it means that i wont see the contents when i go to Computer --> FileSystem --> shared?

---

### Post by NeoLithium on 2007-06-15
I would just create a desktop launcher for it, and in the command option, type in: ```
file:///shared
``` Name it what you like and pick yourself a nice icon. Should open nautifuls up to /shared when you double click on it.

---

### Post by Inxsible on 2007-06-15
> **NeoLithium said:**
> I would just create a desktop launcher for it, and in the command option, type in: ```
file:///shared
``` Name it what you like and pick yourself a nice icon. Should open nautifuls up to /shared when you double click on it.

true, but if and when i change my theme, the icons wouldnt update with the others.

Also i get this error when i do that. check screenie

---

### Post by Inxsible on 2007-06-15
I was just trying stuff out and i changed the launcher command to ```
nautilus /shared
``` and it worked.

thanks NeoLithium,

I would still like to mount it like a drive however, if possible.

---

### Post by NeoLithium on 2007-06-15
Does using:
```
 nautilus --no-desktop /shared
```
Work instead?

---

### Post by Inxsible on 2007-06-15
> **NeoLithium said:**
> Does using:
```
 nautilus --no-desktop /shared
```Work instead?

this worked too. but what's  the --no-desktop for ? It even worked without it.
